task: F0064 wide-load GEMV rewrite - kernel-level microbench, all three legs (COMPLETE; supersedes the blocked-run version of this file)

legs.leg0: pre-F0064 baseline (two strided 32-bit __half2 loads per 4-half chunk)
legs.v1: F0064 default (one 64-bit int2 load per 4-half chunk)
legs.v2: F0064 + -DRWKV7_GEMV_KUNROLL2 (K-unroll x2 load-hoist), flag verified in both build.ninja

prediction_preregistered: F0060 fast-card thesis: 3090's 936 GB/s bus already saturated by narrow-load kernels -> 3090 gain small (+0~3%); 5090 projection ~+9%. Written down before any V1/V2 numbers existed.
correctness: ALL gates PASS with zero differing bytes on BOTH legs: test_mega_rkv + test_mega_a2 (rkv G=3 / o G=1 / rkvo G=4) + test_mega_o_model (real weights) + sqrelu oracle (gemv_m1_sqrelu==relu(gemv_m1)^2) + the independent fixed-seed golden captured pre-swap (gemv_m1 + gemv_m1_sqrelu outputs byte-identical across leg0/V1/V2) + greedy smokes 1.5B 24/24, 7.2B 8/8 EXACT on both legs. Second-arch (sm86) bit-exact re-gate: DONE, PASS.

round1_grouped_gemv_graphed_us_per_call.note: grp = gemv_grouped_m1 one launch; sep = 3x gemv_m1 launches (gemv_m1 is ALSO rewritten by F0064 r2, so sep moves too). Two independent runs per leg (test_mega_rkv + test_mega_a2) shown as run1/run2.

round2_standalone_graphed_us_per_call.units: microseconds per call, cuda-graph replay (production-relevant); odd-N/small-K are sub-2us edge shapes where the deltas are launch-noise, not signal

e2e_bsz1_tok_per_s.protocol: bench/bsz_throughput.py c=1, 64-in/256-out, n=8, identical config per model across all legs

verdict: PREDICTION CONFIRMED - thesis HOLDS. Every reproducible delta sits inside the pre-registered +0~3% band: the largest kernel-level win is -2.4~-2.6% us/call (~+2.5% speed) on the square 4096x4096 GEMV shapes, e2e moves are +0.0~+0.8%, and the single biggest-bytes kernel (7.2B ffn-k 16384x4096) is DEAD FLAT - it is already at the narrow-load kernels' achievable bandwidth on this bus, exactly as F0060 predicts. Mechanistic detail consistent with the thesis: the rewrite only helps where latency-hiding is imperfect (square shapes, fewer rows in flight per wave); the fat shape that saturates the bus gains nothing. V2-vs-V1 on sm86 is noise everywhere (within run-to-run band; e2e 7.2B +0.8% vs V1's +0.0% is at the band edge, not a clear KUNROLL2 win) - so extra loads-in-flight buys nothing measurable on this arch either, again consistent with bus-limited (not latency-limited) behavior at these shapes. The 5090's projected ~+9% remains plausible and untested by this run; nothing here contradicts it.
